title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 Contract · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers node 24, typescript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 contract into a new typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Choose one amqplib promise or callback API and account for RPC methods, event emitters, callback-only confirms, and non-RPC methods.
Treat delivered bytes, fields, properties, headers, and decoded payload as untrusted runtime data despite TypeScript declarations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Runtime Client Version
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 7025 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a typed amqplib client without treating types or publish booleans as broker proof; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Runtime Client Version.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pin node 24.18, npm 12, typescript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, amqp 0-9-1, and rabbitmq 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Runtime Client Version, keep Runtime Client Version connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 1977 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a typed amqplib client without treating types or publish booleans as broker proof; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove clean install, strict type checking, deterministic tests, compiled artifact, connection configuration, and graceful teardown under changed inputs.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Clean Client Build.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prove clean install, strict type checking, deterministic tests, compiled artifact, connection configuration, and graceful teardown under changed inputs. content.value: amqplib 2.0.1 Channel API — Controls promise and callback APIs, connection and channel events, declarations, publishing, drain, returns, consume, ack, nack, prefetch, confirm callbacks, and waitForConfirms.
